nameMeanings.name: Stewart
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish (Lanarkshire) and English: originally an occupational name for an administrative official of an estate, from Middle English stiward, Old English stigweard, stīweard, a compound of stig ‘house(hold)’ + weard ‘guardian’. In the Anglo-Saxon period this title was used of an officer controlling the domestic affairs of a household, especially of the royal household; after the Norman Conquest it was also used more widely as the native equivalent of Seneschal, for the steward of a manor or manager of an estate. In Scotland the term was also used of a magistrate originally appointed by the king to administer crown lands, forming a stewartry.

History: Stuart or Stewart is the surname of one of the great families of Scotland, the royal family of Scotland from the 14th century, and of England from 1603, when James VI of Scotland acceded to the English throne as James I. There were many minor branches of the family left in Britain after the flight of James II in 1688, but not every bearer of the surname can claim relationship with the royal house, even in Scotland. Every great house in medieval England and Scotland had its steward, and in many cases the office gave rise to a hereditary surname. The fall of the house of Stuart in Britain, conversely, led to the establishment of several highly placed branches bearing this surname in continental Europe, which are in most cases related to the old Scottish royal family.

variants.name: Stuart
meanings: Scottish (Aberdeenshire and Banffshire): variant of   Stewart  . This French form of the name, there being no W in the French language, was adopted by Mary, Queen of Scots, on her return to Scotland in 1561 and has since come to be more popular among royalists than the earlier spellings.
History: Stuart or Stewart is the surname of one of the great families of Scotland (see note at   Stewart  ).

variants.name: Stewardson
meanings: English (Lancashire and Westmorland): patronymic from Middle English stiward ‘steward’ (see Stewart ) + -son ‘son of the steward’.

variants.name: Steward
meanings: English (London and East Anglia): variant of   Stewart  .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

nameMeanings.name: Douglas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Transferred use of the surname borne by one of the most powerful families in Scotland, the earls of Douglas and of Angus, also notorious in earlier times as Border reivers. In the 17th and 18th centuries it was used as a girl's name in northern England. It is now exclusively a boys' name, used throughout the English‐speaking world.

en.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret Douglas Stewart was born in 1503. She had at least 1 son with James Maitland. She died in 1535, in Scotland, at the age of 32.

fr.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret Douglas Stewart est née en 1503. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils avec James Maitland. Elle est décédée en 1535, à Écosse, à l’âge de 32 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret Douglas Stewart wurde 1503 geboren. Sie hatte mit James Maitland mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb 1535 im Alter von 32 Jahren in Scotland.

it.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret Douglas Stewart è nata il nel 1503. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlio con James Maitland. Morì nel 1535, nel Scotland, all'età di 32 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret Douglas Stewart(은)는 1503년 태어났다. 그녀는 James Maitland(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1535년 스컷랜드에서 32세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret Douglas Stewart nasceu em 1503. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filho com James Maitland. Ela faleceu em 1535, em Scotland, com 32 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret Douglas Stewart родилась в 1503 г. У неё был по крайней мере 1 сын от James Maitland. Она умерла в 1535 г. в местности Шотландия в возрасте 32 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret Douglas Stewart nació en 1503. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con James Maitland. Murió en 1535, en Scotland, a la edad de 32 años.
